pattern.use_when: charts, KPIs, summaries, multi-engine aggregates, page-level dashboard data
pattern.never: paginated raw findings tables, user mutations (suppress/delete/update)
pattern.cache: yes — TTL set per BFF handler (TTL_SHORT, TTL_CIEM, etc.)

pattern.use_when: paginated findings tables, raw records, drill-down detail, scan history
pattern.never: aggregated data needing multiple engines — use fetchView instead
pattern.auth: access_token cookie + X-Active-Tenant-Id header (forwarded by ingress)

pattern.use_when: user mutations: suppress finding, create rule, trigger scan, update config
partial updates: update schedule, patch rule status
delete rule, remove account, revoke suppression
user management, tenant CRUD, auth operations, SSO — Django platform layer only
pattern.never: engine data, findings, scan results — use engine patterns above

tenant_resolution.step: X-Active-Tenant-Id header (UI localStorage → set by tenant-context.js)
tenant_resolution.critical: BFF resolve_tenant_id() patches the resolved tenant into X-Auth-Context BEFORE forwarding to engines
Never bypass tenant resolution — wrong tenant_id silently returns another tenant's data

auth_flow.step: UI sends access_token cookie (HttpOnly) + X-Active-Tenant-Id header on every request
Gateway AuthMiddleware validates cookie → builds AuthContext → sets X-Auth-Context header
BFF handler reads X-Auth-Context → calls resolve_tenant_id() → patches engine_tenant_id
Engine reads X-Auth-Context → require_permission() check → tenant-scoped DB query
auth_flow.never: DEV_BYPASS_AUTH — permanently removed, never re-add under any circumstance
Skip X-Auth-Context header — engines return 401 or serve wrong-tenant data silently
Add fallback/mock data in BFF when engine returns empty — fix the pipeline instead

entry.note: BFF views and billing/platformAdmin route here

ingress_stripping.note: nginx ingress strips prefix before forwarding to engine K8s svc
